query_model: arbitrary-start windows, 5 starts x {15,30,60,90,full}s, real amateur AMT notes
verdict_automated_note: decision.py emits KILL via the dtw_ceiling<0.70 arm, but that arm's premise (DTW=ceiling) is FALSE here: C1 chroma beats C3 DTW at every window. The KILL is a gate bug, not the real conclusion.

open_set.oracle: C1 note-chroma cosine, full-piece query, leave-one-out negatives (n=16)
open_set.conclusion: Global pitch-class chroma cannot separate correct piece from harmonically-similar wrong piece. Neither absolute cosine nor top1-top2 margin yields an FA<=0.05 @ TA>=0.60 operating point. Open-set rejection via chroma alone is infeasible.

findings: Modality diagnosis CONFIRMED: note-to-note recall recovered from 0.26 cross-modal floor to C1 R@10=0.938 / R@1=0.875 (full piece).
C1 note-chroma wins ranking decisively, is ~free (0.0003s/query), and is highly corruption-robust (R@10 0.875 under severe note errors).
C2 landmark hashing FALSIFIED: R@1=0.000 at every window, collapses to 0.000 under any corruption. Do NOT build it.
C3 DTW-ceiling and C4 chroma-seq-DTW are slower AND worse than C1; C3 is not a real ceiling.
OPEN-SET REJECTION FAILS for chroma (absolute and margin) -- the blocking issue. A wrong lock poisons the session, so C1 alone is not shippable.
Recall climbs strongly with window length (C1 R@10: 0.577@15s -> 0.908@90s -> 0.938@full); more notes = more discriminative, as predicted.
phase1_recommendation: Use C1 note-chroma for recall (top-K), but open-set accept/reject needs a different signal than chroma similarity. Natural candidate: align the top-1 chroma candidate via the existing onset+pitch DTW score-follower (dtw_confirm.rs already exists) and threshold the alignment cost as the unknown-gate -- DTW on ONE candidate is cheap, unlike the 254x standalone C3 measured here. This must be validated (DTW-confirm as open-set gate on C1 top-1) before committing Phase-1 Rust.
harness_defects_found: decision.py KILL arm keys on dtw_ceiling<0.70 assuming DTW is the discrimination ceiling; empirically C1 beats C3, so the arm misfires. Gate should not treat DTW as the ceiling.
run() was silent during ~hours of compute (no progress) -- added per-phase stderr logging.

corruption sweep (secondary diagnostic) runs the slow C3/C4 on full-length recordings BEFORE the primary open-set gate; ordering should put the primary gate first and bound/skip slow matchers in corruption.
